steps.user_prompt: I want to build a chess engine in APL programming language... at the end, I want to test this chess engine and assess its Elo rating, typically by playing games against chess engines of "similar" levels
bash_samples: which apl || true; which gnu-apl || true; which dyalog || true; which dyalogscript || true
which stockfish || true; which cutechess-cli || true; which python3 || true; which uv || true

steps.user_prompt: hum I want a chess engine purely in APL... right now, the main logic is written in Python, with a small call to APL. It's not what I wanted. Python can be used for UCI, that's it.
bash_samples:
tmp=$(mktemp /tmp/argtest.XXXXXX.apl)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
⎕←⊃⎕ARG
⎕←1↓⎕ARG
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp" -- foo bar baz
rm "$tmp"

tmp=$(mktemp /tmp/funtest.XXXXXX.apl)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
∇Z←ADD A
 Z←A+1
∇
⎕←ADD 41
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp" --OFF
rm "$tmp"

steps.user_prompt: Add perft-style APL correctness tests first
bash_samples: apl -s --safe -f src/apl/engine.apl -- bestmove position startpos depth 1
apl -s --safe -f src/apl/engine.apl -- perft position startpos depth 1

steps.user_prompt: is perft depth-4 possible?
bash_samples: time apl -s --safe -f src/apl/engine.apl -- perft position startpos depth 4

steps.user_prompt: it's correct, so good news... what do you mean by "Replace per-search GNU APL startup with a persistent APL worker, while keeping Python limited to UCI transport." ?
let's go this way
bash_samples:
tmp=$(mktemp /tmp/aplworkerXXXXXX.apl)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
⎕IO←0
∇MAIN;LINE
LOOP:
 LINE←⍞
 ⎕←'ECHO:',LINE
 →(LINE≡'quit')⍴DONE
 →LOOP
DONE:
∇
MAIN
)OFF
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp"

tmp=$(mktemp /tmp/splitXXXXXX.apl)
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
⎕IO←0
LINE←'position startpos moves e2e4 e7e5'
⎕←(' '≠LINE)⊂LINE
)OFF
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp"

steps.user_prompt: go to next natural step
bash_samples:
tmp=/tmp/aplprefix_$$.apl
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
⎕IO←0
A←(⊂'e2e4'),(⊂'e7e5')
B←(⊂'e2e4'),(⊂'e7e5'),(⊂'g1f3')
⎕←A≡2↑B
⎕←2↓B
⎕←⍴2↓B
)OFF
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp"
rm "$tmp"

tmp=/tmp/aplstate_$$.apl
cat > "$tmp" <<'EOF'
⎕IO←0
X←(⊂(⊂'a'),(⊂'b')),(⊂(⊂'c'))
⎕←⍴X
⎕←⍴⊃X[0]
⎕←⊃⊃X[0]
)OFF
EOF
apl -s --safe -f "$tmp"
rm "$tmp"
